purpose: Seeded strategy family catalog for tactical decomposition, compound variant composition, verification, and knowledgebase coverage tracking.

strategic.owns: cross-symbol and portfolio structure, regime classification, trend nomination

tactical.owns: decision-tree shape for one lead: branches, entries, exits, invalidations, recovery ladders, allowed order classes

boundedRangeFamilyDefinitions.note: Seeded research starting bands as min/typical/max. Mutable inside immutable caps; paper positions carry realism penalties. Canonical grounding and citations live in tier-lever-and-bounded-range-reference.md.

vol_shock_regime_band.note: Bear/shock regime when rolling realized vol exceeds median × multiplier; tightens heat and sympathy gates

correlation_health_band.note: Sympathy/pairs require intact equilibrium; Johansen rank tests per johansen_1988_vecm

regime_router_thresholds.note: Hurst/ADX nomination bias; Hamilton/Ang–Bekaert for discrete regime overlays

pairs_spread_zscore_band.note: Entry when normalized spread diverges; exit on convergence (GGR distance-method analogue)

momentum_lookback_band.note: Intraday-scaled formation window; academic 3–12 month horizon mapped to bar count per session profile

child_slice_band.note: Max child size as pct of parent; pairs with participation_rate POV

adverse_selection_bps_band.note: Extra limit offset cushion when informed-trading probability is elevated

is_urgency_scalar_band.note: Higher scalar front-loads AC trajectory; lower scalar spreads participation

recovery_backoff_ms_band.note: Jittered cooldown between cancel/replace attempts; preserves queue priority where venue rules allow

granularityByLayer.research: Family, variant, compound, and lineage detail remain fully visible for package curation and conflict resolution.
granularityByLayer.strategic: Only ranked family candidates, veto signals, and high-signal range seeds should flow downstream.
granularityByLayer.tactical: Variant selection, branch construction, recovery ladders, and bounded range families expand into decision-tree detail.
granularityByLayer.execution_agent: Only precision-safe field mappings, deterministic constraints, and control snapshots should remain.
granularityByLayer.training_feedback: Replay outputs may adjust weights and bounded ranges, but only through traceable deltas inside seeded envelopes.

runtimeControlSurface.policyRule: Specific numeric strategy controls are seeded at runtime from current market data, stock-state evidence, and replay history, but they must remain inside pre-seeded tactics, bounded envelopes, and immutable guardrail contracts.

trendLeadPatterns.name: sector_leader_to_sympathy
trendLeadPatterns.summary: A liquid sector leader moves first, then sympathy names and laggards become eligible once breadth and liquidity confirm.

trendLeadPatterns.name: catalyst_cluster_readthrough
trendLeadPatterns.summary: A catalyst in one or more names creates a readthrough basket across the same subsector or supply chain.

trendLeadPatterns.name: macro_shock_blackout_then_reentry
trendLeadPatterns.summary: Macro releases suppress direct activation initially, then controlled reentry is allowed once dispersion and legality filters settle.

trendLeadPatterns.name: liquidity_sweep_then_reclaim
trendLeadPatterns.summary: Price sweeps a key level, reclaims it, and then either reverts or resumes depending on confirmation quality and breadth.

trendLeadPatterns.name: overnight_discovery_to_regular_session_handoff
trendLeadPatterns.summary: Overnight or extended-hours discovery sets the first thesis, then the regular session either validates, reprices, or rejects that thesis.

families.name: opening_range_breakout
families.summary: Uses opening auction and first-range structure to trade clean directional resolution when participation, spreads, and sector breadth confirm.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer nominates opening regime and leader set; tactical layer binds breakout, retest, and failure branches before execution compilation.

subVariants.name: opening_drive_continuation
subVariants.triggerProfile: Immediate expansion after a clean opening range with strong sector breadth and no unresolved macro blackout.

subVariants.name: opening_range_retest_breakout
subVariants.triggerProfile: First breakout pulls back, holds, and re-accelerates once retest quality and volume confirmation are present.
subVariants.leadPattern: initial breakout followed by orderly pullback to range edge

subVariants.name: opening_auction_absorption_breakout
subVariants.triggerProfile: The opening auction dislocates briefly, absorbs at the range edge, then resolves once spreads normalize and sector breadth stays supportive.
subVariants.leadPattern: auction imbalance absorption then range-edge expansion

families.name: gap_and_go
families.summary: Trades catalyst-backed or leadership-backed opening gaps that sustain through liquidity confirmation and early pullback quality.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer identifies catalyst or leadership gap; tactical layer decomposes continuation, first-pullback, and failure branches.

subVariants.name: small_float_gap_drive
subVariants.triggerProfile: Catalyst-backed small-float or thinner-float setup with explosive premarket expansion and strict guardrails.
subVariants.leadPattern: high relative volume and float-aware catalyst basket

subVariants.name: large_cap_catalyst_gap_continuation
subVariants.triggerProfile: Large-cap catalyst gap where liquidity is strong and sympathy propagation matters as much as raw gap size.

subVariants.name: premarket_discovery_continuation
subVariants.triggerProfile: Premarket discovery establishes support and the regular session validates continuation rather than rejecting it.

families.name: pullback_continuation
families.summary: Trades orderly pullbacks within an already-established directional move once sector, breadth, and structure remain aligned.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer identifies durable intraday leader set; tactical layer binds reclaim, continuation, and structure-failure branches.

subVariants.name: first_pullback_to_vwap_hold
subVariants.triggerProfile: Trend impulse pauses into VWAP or first support and resumes as sector breadth remains firm.
subVariants.leadPattern: trend leader with orderly retrace to benchmark

subVariants.name: sector_confirmation_pullback
subVariants.triggerProfile: A leader pulls back while subsector breadth stays strong and sympathy names remain constructive.

subVariants.name: trend_day_midcycle_reclaim
subVariants.triggerProfile: Late-morning or midday reclaim on a confirmed trend day after a shallow consolidation.

families.name: volatility_compression_breakout
families.summary: Targets range-contraction names that are compressing for directional expansion once volume, breadth, and catalyst context align.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer nominates compression baskets; tactical layer binds breakout, fakeout, and reentry branches.

subVariants.name: midday_nr_breakout
subVariants.triggerProfile: Midday narrow-range structure resolves with renewed volume and sector alignment.

subVariants.name: sector_leader_compression_break
subVariants.triggerProfile: A sector leader compresses after a prior move and breaks again, often re-igniting sympathy names.

subVariants.name: closing_range_release
subVariants.triggerProfile: Late-session compression resolves near the close when trend-day continuation or positioning pressure builds.

families.name: vwap_reversion
families.summary: Fades short-horizon overextension back toward anchored intraday value when there is no strong catalyst-driven trend continuation.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer identifies mean-reverting regime; tactical layer binds entry, scale-out, and trend-abort branches.

subVariants.name: single_name_extension_revert
subVariants.triggerProfile: Single-name move extends too far without new information and begins to mean revert toward anchored value.

subVariants.name: index_etf_mean_revert
subVariants.triggerProfile: Index ETF or basket overextends intraday and broad mean reversion becomes more reliable than single-name idiosyncratic reversal.

subVariants.name: catalyst_fade_reversion
subVariants.triggerProfile: A catalyst move loses confirmation and reversion becomes more likely after the initial event response decays.

families.name: liquidity_sweep_reversal
families.summary: Captures reclaim-based reversals after a stop-run, failed level break, or opening flush once tape quality and breadth allow distinction from true continuation.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer identifies reversal-prone stress regime; tactical layer binds reclaim, continuation-abort, and scale-out branches.

subVariants.name: failed_breakout_fade
subVariants.triggerProfile: Breakout fails quickly, reclaims prior range, and offers reversal entry once quality thresholds are met.

subVariants.name: opening_flush_reclaim
subVariants.triggerProfile: An early flush sweeps a key level and is reclaimed once the opening panic resolves.

subVariants.name: session_level_stop_run
subVariants.triggerProfile: Extended-hours or thin-liquidity stop run reverses once spread and reclaim quality are acceptable.

families.name: market_making
families.summary: Provides passive spread-capture and inventory-management templates for the most liquid names once quote realism and policy support are sufficient.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer should only enable this family in explicitly supported liquid environments; tactical layer binds quote width, inventory skew, and retreat logic.

subVariants.name: passive_two_sided_quote
subVariants.triggerProfile: Standard two-sided quoting in stable spread conditions with tight inventory guardrails.

subVariants.name: spread_capture_dislocation
subVariants.triggerProfile: Temporary but orderly spread widening creates spread-capture opportunity without toxic-flow escalation.

subVariants.name: inventory_skew_defense
subVariants.triggerProfile: Inventory imbalance forces skew or retreat logic rather than pure spread-capture continuation.

families.name: pairs_stat_arb
families.summary: Mean-reversion or dispersion strategy for liquid correlated pairs or baskets when correlation, hedge model freshness, and event cleanliness are all acceptable.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer identifies stable spread candidates; tactical layer binds entry, decay, and correlation-break branches.

subVariants.name: intra_sector_pair_mean_revert
subVariants.triggerProfile: Two highly correlated names within a sector diverge temporarily without catalyst contamination.

subVariants.name: basket_dispersion_rebalance
subVariants.triggerProfile: Subsector or basket dispersion becomes large enough to fade relative outliers against a basket anchor.

subVariants.name: event_hedged_pair
subVariants.triggerProfile: One leg has an event but the other is chosen as hedge after explicit event-contamination controls are passed.

families.name: lead_lag_propagation
families.summary: Trades sympathy or lagged response names after a verified leader impulse within the same sector, subsector, basket, or cross-asset theme.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer identifies a trusted leader and sympathy map; tactical layer binds lag window, confirmation, and leader-failure abort branches.

subVariants.name: sector_leader_sympathy
subVariants.triggerProfile: A liquid sector leader breaks or gaps and sympathy names begin repricing with a measurable lag.

subVariants.name: index_constituent_sympathy_rotation
subVariants.triggerProfile: A high-weight index constituent move propagates into correlated peers or suppliers after initial price discovery.

subVariants.name: cross_asset_beta_lag
subVariants.triggerProfile: A commodity, rates, or crypto benchmark impulse propagates into equity proxies with delay.

families.name: earnings_guidance_drift
families.summary: Trades post-event repricing and drift after confirmed earnings, guidance, or similarly high-quality company-event catalysts.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer establishes catalyst quality and readthrough map; tactical layer binds drift, failure, and post-call reversal branches.

subVariants.name: post_earnings_drift_continuation
subVariants.triggerProfile: A confirmed earnings event reprices the name and drift continues through the first actionable session window.

subVariants.name: guidance_sympathy_basket_rotation
subVariants.triggerProfile: Guidance change in a leader causes repricing across a linked subsector or supply-chain basket.

subVariants.name: post_call_repricing
subVariants.triggerProfile: Conference call or follow-up disclosure changes the initial event interpretation and produces a second trade window.

families.name: extended_overnight_session_response
families.summary: Trades short-horizon session-specific opportunities in extended or overnight hours while respecting thin-liquidity and order-type legality constraints.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer sets session posture; tactical layer binds limit-reprice, defer, and regular-session handoff branches before execution compilation.

subVariants.name: overnight_limit_reprice
subVariants.triggerProfile: Overnight thesis remains valid but requires measured limit repricing as spreads and venue state evolve.

subVariants.name: extended_hours_discovery_continuation
subVariants.triggerProfile: Extended-hours move has enough liquidity and catalyst support to remain actionable before the regular open.

subVariants.name: regular_session_transition_handoff
subVariants.triggerProfile: An overnight or extended-hours thesis is carried into the regular session only if the opening cross validates it.

families.name: volatility_regime_overlay
families.summary: Adjusts participation, heat, and family eligibility when realized-vol or implied-vol regime expands or compresses relative to the seeded portfolio vol target band.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer applies overlay weights to nominated families; tactical layer binds vol-scaled stops and participation caps before compile.

subVariants.name: vol_compression_precursor
subVariants.triggerProfile: Vol compresses below target while breadth remains stable—raises compression-breakout family bias.

subVariants.name: vol_expansion_de_risk
subVariants.triggerProfile: Realized vol spikes above target—suppresses pyramiding and tightens heat bands.

families.name: macro_relative_value_dispersion
families.summary: Fades macro-driven sector or factor dispersion when correlation health and event cleanliness support mean reversion across rates-sensitive baskets.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer identifies post-macro dispersion baskets; tactical layer binds entry, decay, and correlation-break exits.

subVariants.name: post_cpi_sector_dispersion_fade
subVariants.triggerProfile: After CPI blackout, sector gaps remain wide while index vol decays.

subVariants.name: rates_factor_pair_rv
subVariants.triggerProfile: Duration-sensitive pair diverges without idiosyncratic catalyst contamination.

families.name: systematic_momentum_burst
families.summary: Captures short-horizon relative-strength bursts when volume acceleration, breadth, and regime classification align on momentum bias.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer requires momentum regime consensus; tactical layer binds burst entry, quick scale-out, and failure abort.

subVariants.name: breadth_thrust_continuation
subVariants.triggerProfile: Market-wide breadth thrust with sector leader follow-through.

subVariants.name: single_name_rvol_burst
subVariants.triggerProfile: Isolated high relative-volume burst with sector confirmation.

families.name: infrastructure_stress_reversal
families.summary: Controlled reclaim or reversion after regulatory, credit, or venue-stress dislocations once official sources, halts, and tape quality confirm a stabilizing flush.

trendLeadBindings.handoffExpectation: Strategic layer requires active event-impact playbook; tactical layer binds reclaim entry, risk-off abort, and limit-only extended rules.

subVariants.name: regulatory_shock_reclaim
subVariants.triggerProfile: Regulatory action gaps a name; reclaim after official-source and halt clearance.

subVariants.name: credit_stress_flush_reversal
subVariants.triggerProfile: Systemic stress flush with defensive rotation stabilizing.

compoundStrategies.name: catalyst_gap_continuation_to_pullback_reclaim
compoundStrategies.summary: Begins with a catalyst-backed gap, validates the first impulse, then hands off to a pullback continuation branch if the retest holds.

compoundStrategies.name: sector_leader_breakout_to_sympathy_rotation
compoundStrategies.summary: A sector leader breaks out first, then sympathy names are traded through lead-lag propagation or pullback continuation as breadth expands.

compoundStrategies.name: macro_shock_blackout_then_delayed_reentry_reversion
compoundStrategies.summary: Direct activation is suppressed during the macro shock window, then delayed reentry looks for mean reversion or reclaim once dispersion normalizes.

compoundStrategies.name: earnings_drift_to_afterhours_response
compoundStrategies.summary: Earnings drift remains actionable through extended or overnight windows only when catalyst freshness and session legality stay intact.

compoundStrategies.name: sweep_reclaim_to_vwap_reversion_failover
compoundStrategies.summary: A reclaim-based reversal is attempted first; if followthrough is weak, the branch falls back to a controlled reversion-to-value exit logic.

compoundStrategies.name: opening_drive_to_sector_sympathy_ladder
compoundStrategies.summary: An opening leader resolves cleanly, then sympathy names rotate through lead-lag propagation and pullback continuation as breadth broadens across the sector.

decisionTreeBranchTaxonomy.purpose: Canonical branch shapes the tactical tier instantiates when decomposing a lead into a decision tree. Every leaf must carry an invalidation distinct from its hard stop and a recovery-ladder ref.
constructionRules: tactical tier must emit at least primary entry, scaled exit, thesis invalidation, time stop, and recovery branches before compile admission
each branch declares the order classes it may compile into; execution tier may only narrow that set
branch weights are mutable inside seeded envelopes; branch existence requirements are not

recoveryLadderTemplates.name: phased_slippage_resolution
recoveryLadderTemplates.rule: on timeout, verify-and-confirm before any resend; blind resend is prohibited

recoveryLadderTemplates.name: breakout_failure_recovery
recoveryLadderTemplates.rule: max_failed_retest_count caps re-entries; halt or macro-aftershock vetoes force lockout

recoveryLadderTemplates.name: reversion_trend_day_abort
recoveryLadderTemplates.rule: trend_strength_veto and benchmark_freshness_threshold force abort over re-entry

recoveryLadderTemplates.name: event_catalyst_recovery
recoveryLadderTemplates.rule: secondary disclosure conflict or overnight policy disallow forces controlled exit, not re-add

recoveryLadderTemplates.name: sympathy_leader_failure_recovery
recoveryLadderTemplates.rule: broken correlation or leader failure aborts the stack; no laggard re-add after abort

recoveryLadderTemplates.name: implementation_shortfall_trajectory_recovery
recoveryLadderTemplates.rule: Re-align child slices toward Almgren–Chriss efficient-frontier trajectory before escalating; Perold IS measured against arrival price

deterministicToolCatalog.purpose: Flexible index of deterministic pipeline tools, tier-scoped lever tools, and executable-state transitions the model-bearing tiers may invoke inside seeded envelopes.

almgren_chriss_optimal_execution.citation: Almgren, R., & Chriss, N. (2000). Optimal execution of portfolio transactions. Journal of Risk, 3(2), 5–39.

bertsimas_lo_1998.citation: Bertsimas, D., & Lo, A. W. (1998). Optimal control of execution costs. Journal of Financial Markets, 1(1), 1–50.

perold_1988_is.citation: Perold, A. F. (1988). The implementation shortfall approach to trading. Journal of Portfolio Management, 14(4), 4–17.

moskowitz_ooi_pedersen_2012_tsmom.citation: Moskowitz, T. J., Ooi, Y. H., & Pedersen, L. H. (2012). Time series momentum. Journal of Financial Economics, 104(2), 228–250.

jegadeesh_titman_1993_csmom.citation: Jegadeesh, N., & Titman, S. (1993). Returns to buying winners and selling losers. Journal of Finance, 48(1), 65–91.

asness_moskowitz_pedersen_2013.citation: Asness, C., Moskowitz, T. J., & Pedersen, L. H. (2013). Value and momentum everywhere. Journal of Finance, 68(3), 929–985.

hamilton_1989_regime_switching.citation: Hamilton, J. D. (1989). A new approach to the economic analysis of nonstationary time series and the business cycle. Econometrica, 57(2), 357–384.

ang_bekaert_2002_regime_switching.citation: Ang, A., & Bekaert, G. (2002). International asset allocation with regime shifts. Review of Financial Studies, 15(4), 1137–1187.

engle_granger_johansen_cointegration.citation: Engle, R. F., & Granger, C. W. J. (1987); Johansen, S. (1988). Cointegration and VECM rank tests.

gatev_ggr_2006_pairs.citation: Gatev, E., Goetzmann, W. N., & Rouwenhorst, K. G. (2006). Pairs trading: Performance of a relative-value arbitrage rule. Review of Financial Studies, 19(3), 797–827.

cointegration_mean_reversion.citation: Engle–Granger (1987) and Johansen (1988) OU spread mean reversion on cointegrated residuals.

kyle_1985_microstructure.citation: Kyle, A. S. (1985). Continuous auctions and insider trading. Econometrica, 53(6), 1315–1335.

glosten_milgrom_1985_adverse_selection.citation: Glosten, L. R., & Milgrom, P. R. (1985). Bid, ask and transaction prices in a specialist market. Journal of Financial Economics, 14(1), 71–100.

cartea_jaimungal_penalva_2015.citation: Cartea, Á., Jaimungal, S., & Penalva, J. (2015). Algorithmic and High-Frequency Trading. Cambridge University Press.

pov_vwap_twap_is.citation: Practitioner execution benchmarks (VWAP, TWAP, POV) framed by Almgren–Chriss IS minimization.

implementation_shortfall.citation: Perold (1988) IS metric; Almgren–Chriss (2000) optimal trajectory.

kelly_1956_criterion.citation: Kelly, J. L. (1956). A new interpretation of information rate. Bell System Technical Journal, 35(4), 917–926.

moreira_muir_2017_vol_managed.citation: Moreira, L., & Muir, T. (2017). Volatility-managed portfolios. Journal of Financial Economics, 124(1), 22–54.

grinold_kahn_2000_active_risk.citation: Grinold, R. C., & Kahn, R. N. (2000). Active Portfolio Management (2nd ed.). McGraw-Hill.

guidolin_timmermann_2007_regime.citation: Guidolin, M., & Timmermann, A. (2007). Asset allocation under multivariate regime switching. Journal of Economic Dynamics and Control, 31(11), 3503–3544.

obizhaeva_wang_2013_optimal_trading.citation: Obizhaeva, A. A., & Wang, J. (2013). Optimal trading strategy and supply/demand dynamics. Review of Financial Studies, 26(8), 3184–3220.

gatheral_2010_market_impact.citation: Gatheral, J. (2010). No-dynamic-arbitrage and market impact. Quantitative Finance, 10(7), 749–759.

almgren_et_al_2005_impact.citation: Almgren, R., Thum, C., Hauptmann, E., & Li, H. (2005). Direct estimation of equity market impact. Risk, July 2005, 58–62.

roll_1984_effective_spread.citation: Roll, R. (1984). A simple implicit measure of the effective bid-ask spread in an efficient market. Journal of Finance, 39(4), 1127–1139.

hasbrouck_1991_information_share.citation: Hasbrouck, J. (1991). Measuring the information content of stock trades. Journal of Finance, 46(1), 179–207.

amihud_2002_illiquidity.citation: Amihud, Y. (2002). Illiquidity and stock returns: cross-section and time-series effects. Journal of Financial Markets, 5(1), 31–56.

krauss_2017_pairs_review.citation: Krauss, C. (2017). Statistical arbitrage pairs trading strategies: Review and outlook. Journal of Economic Surveys, 31(2), 513–545.

avellaneda_lee_2010_stat_arb.citation: Avellaneda, M., & Lee, J.-H. (2010). Statistical arbitrage in the US equities market. Quantitative Finance, 10(7), 761–782.

lo_mackinlay_1988_variance_ratio.citation: Lo, A. W., & MacKinlay, A. C. (1988). Stock market prices do not follow random walks: Evidence from a simple specification test. Review of Financial Studies, 1(1), 41–66.

mandelbrot_wallis_1969_hurst.citation: Mandelbrot, B. B., & Wallis, J. R. (1969). Robust R/S analysis of long run serial correlation. Proceedings of the 37th Session of the ISI, 59–104.

back_baruch_2004_kyle_gm.citation: Back, K., & Baruch, S. (2004). Information in securities markets: Kyle meets Glosten and Milgrom. Econometrica, 72(2), 433–465.

easley_ohara_1992_price_adjustment.citation: Easley, D., & O'Hara, M. (1992). Time and the process of security price adjustment. Journal of Finance, 47(2), 577–604.

easley_lopez_prado_ohara_2012_vpin.citation: Easley, D., López de Prado, M. M., & O'Hara, M. (2012). Flow toxicity and liquidity in a high-frequency world. Review of Financial Studies, 25(5), 1457–1493.

engle_granger_1987_cointegration.citation: Engle, R. F., & Granger, C. W. J. (1987). Co-integration and error correction. Econometrica, 55(2), 251–276.

johansen_1988_vecm.citation: Johansen, S. (1988). Statistical analysis of cointegration vectors. Journal of Economic Dynamics and Control, 12(2–3), 231–254.

strategic.note: Hurst/ADX thresholds; Hamilton/Guidolin overlays via run_regime_switch_calibration
GGR distance pre-screen; cointegration mandatory per engle_granger_1987_cointegration

execution.note: Glosten–Milgrom spread cushion; VPIN-style toxicity proxy per easley_lopez_prado_ohara_2012_vpin

processBindingTools.tierEffect: Fixes which pipeline tool ids run per NodeKind for the LLM-selected pattern
Materializes tactical tree branches for lever attachment
Maps execution-tier bands to compiled instruction ref
Repositions levers on same tree; may enqueue retune_tree
Fail-closed lever application for LLM-proposed deltas
